reasoning: The Lean encoding captures each part of the informal statement accurately: "odd primes" is expressed as `p.Prime ∧ p > 2` (and similarly for q), which is equivalent since 2 is the only even prime; "distinct" is `p ≠ q`; "p−1 divides q−1" is `p - 1 ∣ q - 1` (well-defined natural subtraction since p, q > 2); "(n, pq) = 1" is `n.gcd (p*q) = 1`; and the conclusion `n^(q-1) ≡ 1 [MOD p*q]` is `Nat.ModEq` for congruence modulo pq. Working in ℕ for n rather than ℤ does not weaken or strengthen the statement, since every integer residue class mod pq has a natural representative and gcd is preserved. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
